title: Do Wren Kitchens Install Their Own Kitchens?
metaDescription: Wren offers optional installation but you can use any independent fitter. Install My Kitchen installs Wren kitchens independently across Coventry and Warwickshire — call 07399 651836.
answer: Wren Kitchens offers an optional installation service through approved fitters, but you are not required to use it. Any qualified independent kitchen fitter can install your Wren kitchen. The unit warranty covers manufacturing defects regardless of who fits it.
bodyHtml:
Wren's Optional Installation Service

Wren Kitchens provides an optional installation service through a network of approved fitters. When you purchase through a Wren showroom, an installation package will be offered. However, this is entirely optional — you are under no obligation to use it. Wren's installation service is simply a convenience offering, not a condition of purchase or warranty.

Using an Independent Fitter for Wren

Any qualified and experienced kitchen fitter can install your Wren kitchen. Wren units are supplied flat-packed or partially assembled and are straightforward for an experienced fitter to work with. The unit warranty covers manufacturing defects and is not invalidated by using an independent fitter. What matters is that the installation is carried out to a professional standard.

Independent fitters often offer more flexible scheduling and more competitive pricing than retailer-arranged services, and their only loyalty is to you rather than the retailer.

Wren Kitchen Installation in Coventry

Install My Kitchen installs Wren kitchens across Coventry and Warwickshire. We have no affiliation with Wren, which means our focus is entirely on your finished kitchen. Contact Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836 or visit us at 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry, CV6 2AH to discuss your project.

faqs.q: Does using an independent fitter void my Wren warranty?
faqs.a: No. The Wren unit warranty covers manufacturing defects regardless of who installs the kitchen. Installation workmanship is a separate matter covered by your fitter's own guarantee.

faqs.q: How do I get a quote for independent Wren installation?
faqs.a: Contact Install My Kitchen on 07399 651836. We will arrange a pre-installation survey at your home for £195, credited back when you book the installation.

faqs.q: Are Wren kitchens easy to fit compared to other brands?
faqs.a: Wren kitchens are well-designed and come with detailed fitting instructions. For an experienced fitter they present no unusual challenges. The carcasses are rigid and the components are generally consistent in quality.

faqs.q: Can I use an independent fitter if I have already paid Wren for installation?
faqs.a: You would need to cancel Wren's installation service and request a refund for that element. Check your order terms. Contact Wren directly before engaging an independent fitter.

title: Does Howdens Do Kitchen Installation?
metaDescription: Howdens sells trade-only and has no consumer installation service. Independent fitters are the standard route. Install My Kitchen installs Howdens kitchens across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: Howdens does not offer a consumer installation service — they sell trade-only, so customers arrange installation independently. Most Howdens kitchens are installed by independent fitters or builders. Install My Kitchen provides independent Howdens kitchen installation across Coventry and Warwickshire.
bodyHtml:
The Howdens Trade-Only Model

Howdens Joinery operates exclusively on a trade basis. Members of the public cannot purchase directly from a Howdens depot — all orders must be placed through a tradesperson who holds a Howdens account. This is a fundamental part of the Howdens business model and means that Howdens does not offer a consumer-facing installation service of any kind.

Who Installs Howdens Kitchens

The tradesperson who places your Howdens order may or may not be the person fitting your kitchen. Many builders, kitchen fitters, and project managers use Howdens as their preferred supplier and will order and install on your behalf. If you have already purchased through a Howdens account holder, you still have complete freedom to choose your own fitter for the installation.

Howdens units are rigid and pre-assembled, which makes them efficient to install. They are widely regarded by fitters as a reliable, consistent product.

Independent Howdens Installation in Coventry

Install My Kitchen installs Howdens kitchens independently across Coventry and Warwickshire. We work with all Howdens ranges and can co-ordinate with your Howdens account holder if needed. Call Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836 or visit 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry, CV6 2AH.

faqs.q: Can I buy from Howdens without a trade account?
faqs.a: No. Howdens sells exclusively to trade account holders. If you want a Howdens kitchen, you will need a builder, kitchen fitter, or project manager who holds a Howdens account to order on your behalf.

faqs.q: Does Howdens have a showroom I can visit?
faqs.a: Howdens has local depots rather than consumer showrooms. You can visit a depot with your tradesperson to view samples, but you cannot order independently without a trade account.

faqs.q: Are Howdens kitchens good quality?
faqs.a: Howdens kitchens are widely regarded as excellent value for money. The rigid carcasses are sturdy, the hinges and drawer runners are reliable, and the range covers everything from budget to premium finishes.

faqs.q: How do I get my Howdens kitchen installed in Coventry?
faqs.a: Contact Install My Kitchen on 07399 651836. We install all Howdens ranges and can discuss your project, carry out a pre-installation survey, and provide a fixed quote.

title: Does Magnet Kitchens Do Installation?
metaDescription: Magnet offers installation through showrooms but you can use any independent fitter. Install My Kitchen installs all Magnet ranges across Coventry and Warwickshire independently.
answer: Magnet offers an installation service through their showrooms, but you are not required to use it. Independent fitters can install any Magnet kitchen without affecting the unit warranty. Install My Kitchen installs all Magnet ranges independently across Coventry and Warwickshire.
bodyHtml:
Magnet's Showroom Installation Service

Magnet Kitchens offer an installation service as part of their showroom package. When you design your kitchen with a Magnet designer, an installation option will be presented. Like most retailer installation services, it is optional — you are purchasing the kitchen, not a combined supply-and-fit contract unless you choose to.

Using an Independent Magnet Fitter

Any experienced kitchen fitter can install a Magnet kitchen. Magnet units are straightforward to work with and their fitting instructions are clear. The unit warranty covers manufacturing defects and is not conditional on using Magnet's own installation service. Using an independent fitter gives you full control over scheduling and pricing.

Independent fitters are accountable directly to you. If something needs adjusting after installation, you contact your fitter — not a distant national service centre.

Magnet Installation in Coventry

Install My Kitchen installs all Magnet ranges independently across Coventry and Warwickshire. We are entirely independent — no retailer affiliation, no quotas, no rush. Pindi Sahota has 30 years of fitting experience and works to a meticulous standard. Call 07399 651836 or visit 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry, CV6 2AH.

faqs.q: Will Magnet's warranty be affected if I use an independent fitter?
faqs.a: No. Magnet's unit warranty covers manufacturing defects and is not conditional on using their installation service. Your fitter's workmanship is covered separately by their own guarantee.

faqs.q: Can I get a separate quote for installation from Magnet?
faqs.a: Yes. Magnet will quote for installation separately to the kitchen purchase. You can compare this with independent fitter quotes before deciding which route to take.

faqs.q: How long does a Magnet kitchen take to install?
faqs.a: Timescales depend on kitchen size. A typical medium Magnet kitchen takes 4–6 days for an experienced fitter. A pre-installation survey will give you a precise timescale for your project.

faqs.q: Does Install My Kitchen fit all Magnet ranges?
faqs.a: Yes. Install My Kitchen installs all Magnet kitchen ranges across Coventry and Warwickshire. Contact us on 07399 651836 to arrange a survey and quote.

title: Does IKEA Do Kitchen Installation in the UK?
metaDescription: IKEA offers installation through a third-party provider, but independent fitters are faster and often cheaper. Install My Kitchen installs IKEA kitchens across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: IKEA UK offers kitchen installation through a third-party provider, but the service often has significant waiting times. Independent kitchen fitters install IKEA METOD kitchens and frequently offer faster availability and more competitive pricing.
bodyHtml:
IKEA's UK Installation Service

IKEA UK offers a kitchen installation service arranged through a third-party provider. This can be booked through the IKEA website alongside your kitchen order. However, the service can involve significant lead times — often several weeks — and the standard of work varies depending on the sub-contracted fitter allocated to your job. You have no direct relationship with the fitter and limited recourse if problems arise.

Why Homeowners Choose Independent IKEA Fitters

Independent kitchen fitters who specialise in IKEA METOD kitchens are widely used across the UK. They typically offer faster availability, fixed pricing, and direct accountability. IKEA kitchens are flat-pack and assembly-intensive — choosing an experienced independent fitter means your kitchen is built efficiently and correctly, with all adjustments made on the day.

Many homeowners find that independent IKEA installation costs less than the IKEA-arranged service and is completed significantly sooner.

IKEA Kitchen Installation in Coventry

Install My Kitchen installs IKEA METOD kitchens across Coventry and Warwickshire. We are familiar with the full IKEA range and all associated accessories. Call Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836 or visit 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry, CV6 2AH.

faqs.q: Does IKEA's installation service cover Coventry?
faqs.a: IKEA's third-party installation service covers most of the UK, including Coventry. However, lead times can be lengthy. An independent fitter is often available sooner and provides more direct accountability.

faqs.q: Are IKEA kitchens difficult to install?
faqs.a: IKEA METOD kitchens are flat-pack and more assembly-intensive than rigid-carcass alternatives. An experienced fitter handles this efficiently, but it does add time compared to installing a Howdens or Wren kitchen.

faqs.q: Does using an independent fitter affect my IKEA product guarantee?
faqs.a: No. IKEA's 25-year structural guarantee covers the kitchen units regardless of who installs them. It is a product guarantee, not a supply-and-fit condition.

faqs.q: How long does an IKEA kitchen take to install?
faqs.a: An IKEA METOD kitchen typically takes 1–2 days longer than an equivalent rigid-carcass kitchen due to the flat-pack assembly. A medium IKEA kitchen might take 5–8 days depending on the number of units and appliances.

title: Can I Install a Kitchen Myself?
metaDescription: DIY kitchen installation is possible but carries significant risk. Unlevel units, misaligned doors and incorrect services are common problems. Professional installation includes a workmanship warranty.
answer: It is possible to install a kitchen yourself with strong DIY skills, but it carries significant risk. Most DIY installations suffer from unlevel units, misaligned doors, and incorrect service positions. Professional installation protects a kitchen investment of thousands of pounds and includes a workmanship warranty.
bodyHtml:
What DIY Kitchen Installation Involves

Installing a kitchen yourself involves far more than assembling flatpack units. You will need to survey the room accurately, plan service positions, level base units across an often uneven floor, hang wall units at a consistent height, cut worktops precisely, and carry out accurate scribing to walls. You will also need to co-ordinate plumbing and electrical work at first and second fix stages. Each of these steps requires specific skills and the right tools.

The Risks of DIY Fitting

The most common problems with DIY kitchen installations are unlevel base units, misaligned doors and drawer fronts, poorly cut worktop joints, and incorrect positioning of sinks and appliances. These faults are often difficult and expensive to correct after the fact — particularly if worktops have already been cut. A poorly fitted kitchen can also affect appliance operation and void appliance warranties.

Why Professional Installation Is Worth the Cost

A professionally installed kitchen protects an investment that typically costs £5,000–£30,000 in units and appliances. A professional fitter carries public liability insurance, provides a workmanship warranty, and can correct problems before handover. The cost of professional installation is a small fraction of the total kitchen spend. Call Install My Kitchen on 07399 651836 to discuss your project.

faqs.q: What tools do I need to install a kitchen myself?
faqs.a: You will need a spirit level, laser level, jigsaw, circular saw, router, drill, multi-tool, silicone gun, and an extensive set of hand tools. Many of these are specialist items that a professional fitter owns and uses daily.

faqs.q: Will DIY installation void my kitchen warranty?
faqs.a: Unit warranties typically cover manufacturing defects and are not affected by who installs the kitchen. However, appliance warranties may be affected if installation does not comply with manufacturer guidelines.

faqs.q: Can I do part of the installation myself and hire a fitter for the rest?
faqs.a: Most professional fitters prefer to carry out a complete installation rather than correct or finish someone else's work. Discuss this honestly with any fitter you contact — they may decline or charge more.

faqs.q: What is the most common DIY kitchen fitting mistake?
faqs.a: Failing to get the first base unit perfectly level. Every subsequent unit is referenced from the first, so any error compounds across the full run of cabinets.

title: What Does a Kitchen Fitter Actually Do?
metaDescription: A kitchen fitter assembles cabinets, fits worktops, hangs doors, installs appliances and completes snagging. Pindi Sahota at Install My Kitchen has 30 years of experience across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: A kitchen fitter assembles and installs all kitchen cabinetry, fits worktops, hangs doors and drawers, installs handles, fits plinths and cornices, positions appliances and sinks, and completes a full snagging check. A good fitter also surveys the room before starting and provides a fixed quote.
bodyHtml:
The Pre-Installation Survey

A professional kitchen fitter begins before installation day with a detailed survey of your room and kitchen plan. This involves checking floor levels across the full room, assessing wall conditions, confirming service positions for plumbing and electrics, reviewing the kitchen plan against the actual space, and identifying any issues that could cause delays. The survey produces a fixed quote and an accurate installation programme. Skipping this step is a significant risk.

Cabinet Installation and Levelling

On installation day, the fitter begins by establishing a datum level and installing base cabinets using adjustable feet. Wall cabinets are hung to a consistent height. Tall units are fixed securely to the wall. Carcasses are joined together for rigidity, and every unit is checked for level and square before worktops are fitted. With 30 years of experience, Pindi Sahota works to a precise standard that eliminates problems at the snagging stage.

Worktops, Doors and Finishing

Once cabinets are installed, worktops are measured, cut, and fitted — including joints, upstands, and sink cut-outs. Doors, drawer fronts, and handles are then fitted and adjusted for perfect alignment. Plinths and cornices are scribed and fixed. Appliances are positioned in their housings. A full snagging check completes the installation before handover to the homeowner.

faqs.q: Does a kitchen fitter supply the kitchen or just fit it?
faqs.a: Most kitchen fitters are installation specialists only — you purchase the kitchen separately from a retailer such as Wren, Howdens, Magnet, or IKEA. Some fitters, including Install My Kitchen, also supply worktops.

faqs.q: Does a kitchen fitter connect the sink and dishwasher?
faqs.a: A kitchen fitter positions the sink and dishwasher within their units, but the water supply and waste connections are carried out by a qualified plumber at second-fix stage.

faqs.q: How many days does a kitchen fitter need to complete a kitchen?
faqs.a: Typically 3–10 days depending on the number of units. A small kitchen takes 3–4 days; a large kitchen takes up to 10 days. Your survey will give you a precise programme.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a kitchen fitter and a joiner?
faqs.a: A kitchen fitter specialises specifically in kitchen installation. A joiner is a broader carpentry trade. Many kitchen fitters hold carpentry or joinery qualifications, but specialist kitchen fitting experience is the most important factor.

title: Is Wren Better Than Howdens? A Fitter's View
metaDescription: Wren and Howdens serve different markets. From 30 years fitting both, the quality difference is minimal. Wren is consumer-facing; Howdens is trade-only. Choice depends on your priorities.
answer: Neither is objectively better — they serve different markets. Wren is consumer-facing with showrooms; Howdens is trade-only with consistent quality. From 30 years fitting both, the quality difference is minimal. The better choice depends on your style preference and whether you have trade access.
bodyHtml:
Supply Model Differences

Wren and Howdens operate completely different supply models. Wren is a consumer-facing retailer — you walk into a showroom, design your kitchen with a designer, and purchase directly. Howdens is trade-only, meaning you need a builder or fitter with a Howdens account to place the order on your behalf. This fundamental difference affects pricing, availability, and the buying experience rather than the quality of the units themselves.

Unit Construction and Quality

Having fitted both brands for over 30 years, the honest answer is that both are competent products. Howdens uses rigid pre-assembled carcasses across most of their range and is known for consistent quality from depot to depot. Wren uses a mix of flat-pack and pre-assembled carcasses depending on the range. Both use similar hinge and drawer runner hardware from established European suppliers. The finish quality on both ranges varies significantly by price point.

Which Is Right for You

If you have trade access through a builder or fitter, Howdens offers excellent value and very reliable stock. If you want a full showroom experience with a designer included, Wren is a strong choice. Either way, the quality of installation matters as much as the brand — a good fitter will produce a great result from either kitchen. Call Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836 for an independent view.

faqs.q: Are Howdens kitchens cheaper than Wren?
faqs.a: Howdens pricing is trade-only and not publicly listed. The effective cost depends on your tradesperson's account terms. Wren has public pricing. Direct comparison is difficult, but both cover a similar price range from entry-level to premium.

faqs.q: Which brand do kitchen fitters prefer to work with?
faqs.a: Many fitters prefer Howdens because the rigid pre-assembled carcasses are quick to install. Wren is also straightforward to work with. Fitter preference rarely affects the finished quality for the homeowner.

faqs.q: Can I mix units from Wren and Howdens?
faqs.a: In theory units from different brands could be mixed in a large kitchen, but this is not recommended. Heights and depths can vary slightly between brands, making a consistent finish very difficult to achieve.

faqs.q: Does Install My Kitchen fit both Wren and Howdens?
faqs.a: Yes. Install My Kitchen installs both Wren and Howdens kitchens, as well as Magnet, IKEA, and most other major brands. Call 07399 651836 to discuss your project.

title: How Long Does Kitchen Installation Take?
metaDescription: Small kitchen: 3-4 days. Medium: 4-6 days. Large: 6-10 days. IKEA adds 1-2 days for flat-pack assembly. Timescales from Install My Kitchen's 30 years of fitting experience.
answer: A small kitchen (8–12 units) takes 3–4 days. A medium kitchen (13–20 units) takes 4–6 days. A large kitchen (21+ units) takes 6–10 days. IKEA flat-pack adds 1–2 days. These timescales assume the room is prepared, services are complete, and delivery is undamaged.
bodyHtml:
Installation Days by Kitchen Size

Kitchen installation time is primarily driven by the number of units and the complexity of the layout. A small kitchen with 8–12 units typically takes 3–4 days for a single experienced fitter. A medium kitchen with 13–20 units takes 4–6 days. A large kitchen with 21 or more units, including island or peninsula sections, typically takes 6–10 days. IKEA METOD kitchens take 1–2 days longer than equivalent rigid-carcass kitchens due to the flat-pack assembly involved.

What Makes a Kitchen Take Longer

Several factors extend installation time beyond these estimates. These include: structural issues such as significantly uneven floors or out-of-square rooms; complex worktop configurations including islands or mitred corners; stone worktops requiring a separate templating visit; appliance integration with cabinetry modification; damaged units requiring retailer replacement; and first-fix services that are not ready on the planned start date.

Planning Your Installation Programme

A pre-installation survey is the most reliable way to get an accurate timescale for your specific project. At Install My Kitchen, the survey visit produces a detailed programme before any installation day is confirmed. This avoids the common problem of underestimating a project and having to extend the booking at short notice. Call Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836 to arrange a survey.

faqs.q: Can two fitters working together halve the installation time?
faqs.a: Not quite. Some tasks — such as hanging wall units — benefit from two people, but many tasks are sequential and cannot be parallelised. Two fitters may reduce time by 30–40% rather than 50%.

faqs.q: Does a kitchen fitter work weekends?
faqs.a: This varies by fitter. At Install My Kitchen we discuss scheduling at the survey stage to find a start date that suits your household. Weekend working can sometimes be arranged.

faqs.q: Do I need to be at home during kitchen installation?
faqs.a: You do not need to be present throughout, but you should be available on the first morning and last afternoon. Being reachable by phone during installation days is important in case decisions need to be made.

faqs.q: What happens if delivery is damaged and units need replacing?
faqs.a: Damaged units must be reported to the retailer immediately. Replacement lead times vary by brand — typically 3–10 working days. Installation must pause until replacements arrive, so prompt reporting is essential.

title: How Much Should Kitchen Fitting Cost in 2025?
metaDescription: Kitchen fitting costs £1,800-£7,500 for labour. Fitters should charge £300-£450 per day. Always get a fixed total price — not just a day rate — from Install My Kitchen.
answer: Kitchen fitting should cost approximately £1,800–£7,500 for labour depending on size. A fitter should charge around £300–£450 per day. Be cautious of quotes below £300/day — either scope is missing or quality will suffer. Always get a fixed total price, not just a day rate.
bodyHtml:
Cost by Kitchen Size

Kitchen fitting labour in 2025 typically costs £1,800–£3,000 for a small kitchen, £3,000–£5,000 for a medium kitchen, and £5,000–£7,500 or more for a large or complex kitchen. These figures cover installation of cabinetry, worktops, and appliance positioning. They do not include first-fix plumbing, electrical work, tiling, or plastering, which are separate trades. Stone worktop fabrication and fitting is also additional.

What a Day Rate Should Include

A professional kitchen fitter should charge £300–£450 per day in 2025. This rate should include all labour, consumables such as silicone and fixings, and use of specialist tools including laser levels and routers. Be cautious of day rates below £300 — this often indicates missing scope, lack of specialist tools, or a fitter who will rush to maintain margin. Very low rates rarely produce professional results on a complex installation.

Why a Fixed Price Matters More Than a Day Rate

A day rate without a fixed total price transfers all risk to you. If installation takes longer than estimated, you pay more. A fixed total price means the fitter absorbs any additional time. Always insist on a written fixed quote following a pre-installation survey. Install My Kitchen provides a fixed price for every installation following our £195 survey visit — credited back on booking. Call 07399 651836.

faqs.q: Why are some kitchen fitting quotes much cheaper than others?
faqs.a: Very cheap quotes often exclude elements you expect to be included, such as worktop fitting or appliance installation. They may also come from fitters without adequate experience or insurance. Always compare quotes on a like-for-like scope.

faqs.q: Is kitchen fitting VAT-rated?
faqs.a: Yes. Kitchen fitting is subject to 20% VAT for VAT-registered businesses. Check whether quotes include or exclude VAT — this can significantly affect the comparison between quotes.

faqs.q: Should I pay a deposit for kitchen installation?
faqs.a: A modest deposit of 10–25% on booking is normal and reasonable. Be cautious of any fitter requesting more than 50% upfront. Never pay the full amount before work begins.

faqs.q: Does Install My Kitchen charge extra for removing the old kitchen?
faqs.a: Strip-out and disposal is quoted separately and typically adds £200–£400 depending on kitchen size. This is itemised clearly in our fixed quote following the pre-installation survey.

title: Do Kitchen Fitters Remove the Old Kitchen?
metaDescription: Strip-out is not always included in installation quotes. Install My Kitchen arranges strip-out and disposal for £200-£400 depending on kitchen size across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: Strip-out is not always included in a standard installation quote. Install My Kitchen can arrange strip-out and disposal as an addition, typically adding £200–£400 to the project cost depending on kitchen size.
bodyHtml:
What Kitchen Strip-Out Involves

Kitchen strip-out involves carefully removing all existing units, worktops, plinths, and cornices without damaging the walls, floor, or services behind them. Appliances must be disconnected by the appropriate trades before removal. The stripped kitchen must then be removed from the property and disposed of responsibly — either via a skip, a licensed waste carrier, or a recycling centre. This is a skilled and physically demanding task that is typically charged separately.

Is Strip-Out Included in My Quote

Strip-out is not automatically included in most kitchen installation quotes. It is commonly itemised separately because the time and disposal cost varies significantly depending on the size of the existing kitchen and access to the property. At Install My Kitchen, strip-out and disposal is quoted as a clear separate line item after the pre-installation survey. Typical additional cost is £200–£400 depending on kitchen size. This avoids any ambiguity on project day.

Asbestos Awareness in Older Kitchens

In properties built before 2000, there is a possibility of asbestos-containing materials in floor tiles, ceiling tiles, or textured coatings behind existing units. If your property is of this age, an asbestos survey is advisable before any strip-out work begins. This is a legal requirement for certain building types and a sensible precaution in any older home. Install My Kitchen can advise on this during the survey visit.

faqs.q: Can I remove my old kitchen myself to save money?
faqs.a: Yes, provided you are confident with the work and all appliances have been disconnected by appropriate trades first. DIY strip-out can save money, but care must be taken not to damage walls, floors, or services that the new kitchen will use.

faqs.q: Who disconnects the existing sink and appliances before strip-out?
faqs.a: The sink and any plumbed appliances must be disconnected by a qualified plumber before strip-out begins. The cooker or hob must be disconnected by a qualified electrician or gas engineer depending on the fuel type.

faqs.q: How is the old kitchen disposed of?
faqs.a: Install My Kitchen arranges disposal through a licensed waste carrier. Units are recycled where possible. The disposal cost is included in the strip-out charge quoted after the survey.

faqs.q: Does strip-out damage the walls?
faqs.a: Professional strip-out minimises wall damage, but it is normal for some areas to require filling and repainting after removal. Tiling behind existing units may also need attention before the new kitchen is installed.

title: Do Kitchen Fitters Do Plumbing?
metaDescription: Kitchen fitters install units but plumbing connections require a qualified plumber. Learn how to co-ordinate both trades for your Coventry kitchen installation.
answer: Kitchen fitters install units but not first or second-fix plumbing — that requires a qualified plumber. Fitters position sinks and dishwashers within units; plumbers connect water supply and waste. Both trades must be co-ordinated around the installation programme.
bodyHtml:
What Kitchen Fitters Do vs Plumbers

A kitchen fitter installs all cabinetry, worktops, and appliances. However, the water supply connections, waste connections, and any new pipework are the domain of a qualified plumber. A kitchen fitter will position the sink within the worktop and the dishwasher within its housing, but making the physical connections to the water supply and drainage requires a qualified plumber. These are distinct trades with distinct responsibilities.

First Fix and Second Fix Explained

Plumbing work in a kitchen renovation happens at two stages. First fix occurs before the kitchen is installed — new supply pipes and waste runs are positioned so that connection points will be in exactly the right place once units are fitted. Second fix occurs after the kitchen fitter has installed the units — the plumber then connects the sink, dishwasher, and any other plumbed appliances. Both stages must be timed correctly around the installation programme.

Co-ordinating Trades for Your Installation

The most common kitchen renovation delays arise from poor co-ordination between the fitter, plumber, and electrician. At Install My Kitchen, we discuss the full programme at the survey stage so you know exactly when each trade is needed. This prevents costly delays where the fitter is waiting on a plumber or vice versa. Contact Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836 to discuss your project.

faqs.q: Can a kitchen fitter move a sink position?
faqs.a: A kitchen fitter can position the sink unit wherever the plan requires, but moving the water supply and waste connections requires a plumber. The two trades work together — the plumber moves the services, then the fitter installs the units.

faqs.q: How much does kitchen plumbing cost on top of fitting?
faqs.a: First and second fix kitchen plumbing typically costs £300–£800 depending on the extent of work. Simple second-fix connection of an existing sink to existing supply positions costs less. New pipework runs cost more.

faqs.q: Do I need a plumber before the kitchen fitter starts?
faqs.a: First-fix plumbing — new pipes and waste runs — must be completed before installation begins. Second-fix connections happen after the fitter has installed units and cut the worktop for the sink. Timing is planned at the survey stage.

faqs.q: Can a gas engineer connect my gas hob?
faqs.a: Yes. Gas hob connections must be carried out by a Gas Safe registered engineer. This is a legal requirement and non-negotiable. Your kitchen fitter will position the hob within its housing; a Gas Safe engineer makes the gas connection.

title: Do Kitchen Fitters Do Electrical Work?
metaDescription: Kitchen fitters fit appliances but hardwired electrical connections require a Part P electrician. Learn how electrical work fits into your kitchen installation programme.
answer: Kitchen fitters fit appliances but do not carry out hardwired electrical connections — that requires a Part P qualified electrician. This includes oven and hob connections and new socket circuits. Fitters and electricians work at different stages of the installation programme.
bodyHtml:
What Requires an Electrician

A kitchen fitter positions and secures all electrical appliances within their housings. However, any hardwired electrical connection — including ovens, electric hobs, extractor fans on a dedicated circuit, and new socket outlets — must be carried out by a Part P qualified electrician. Plugging an appliance into an existing socket is not notifiable work, but new circuits and hardwired connections are. Attempting these connections without the correct qualification is illegal and potentially dangerous.

Part P Certification Explained

Part P of the Building Regulations covers electrical safety in dwellings in England. Notifiable electrical work must be carried out by a registered electrician who can self-certify compliance, or by any competent person with subsequent inspection by a building control officer. Kitchen installation routinely generates notifiable electrical work — new circuits for ovens, hobs, and additional sockets are all notifiable. Always use a Part P registered electrician and obtain a completion certificate for your records.

Scheduling Your Electrician Around Installation

Like plumbing, electrical work happens at two stages — first fix before installation and second fix after. Book your electrician in parallel with your fitter and confirm the programme at the survey stage. Install My Kitchen provides a clear installation programme at survey stage to help you co-ordinate all trades correctly. Call Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836.

faqs.q: Can a kitchen fitter connect my electric oven?
faqs.a: No. Electric oven connections are notifiable electrical work under Part P of the Building Regulations. A Part P qualified electrician must carry out this connection. A kitchen fitter will position the oven in its housing ready for connection.

faqs.q: What is first-fix electrical work in a kitchen?
faqs.a: First-fix electrical work involves running new cables and positioning back boxes for sockets, switches, and appliance connections before the kitchen is installed. It must be completed before the fitter starts so that service positions are correct.

faqs.q: Do I need a completion certificate for kitchen electrical work?
faqs.a: Yes. Any notifiable electrical work including new circuits must be certified by a Part P registered electrician. This certificate is important for building control compliance and is required when you sell the property.

faqs.q: How long does kitchen electrical work take?
faqs.a: First-fix electrical work in a kitchen typically takes half a day to a full day. Second-fix connection of appliances takes a further 2–4 hours depending on the number of appliances. This is planned into the overall installation programme.

title: Do You Need Planning Permission for a New Kitchen?
metaDescription: In most cases no planning permission is needed for a new kitchen. Exceptions include listed buildings and structural changes. Check with Coventry City Council if uncertain.
answer: In most cases, no — fitting a new kitchen in an existing space does not require planning permission. Exceptions include listed buildings where Listed Building Consent may be required, or structural changes to walls or floors. Always check with your local authority if uncertain.
bodyHtml:
When Planning Permission Is Not Needed

Replacing or fitting a new kitchen within an existing room is classified as an interior alteration. Interior alterations that do not affect the external appearance of a building or involve structural changes do not require planning permission in most cases. This applies whether you are replacing an old kitchen with a new one or fitting a kitchen in an existing room that has never had one. The work is permitted development under current planning legislation.

When You May Need Consent

Certain situations require formal consent before kitchen work can begin. If your property is a listed building, Listed Building Consent may be required even for interior work — this applies to all grades of listing. If your kitchen project involves removing or altering a load-bearing wall, structural approval and potentially planning permission may be required. Any extension to create a larger kitchen area would require planning permission in the normal way.

Listed Buildings and Coventry

Coventry has a significant number of listed buildings across the city, including many residential properties in conservation areas. If you are in any doubt about whether consent is required, contact Coventry City Council's planning department or speak to your architect before works begin. Install My Kitchen can advise informally based on the pre-installation survey, but formal planning advice must come from the local authority or a planning consultant.

faqs.q: Does fitting a kitchen extension require planning permission?
faqs.a: Yes. Any extension to your home — including a kitchen extension — requires planning permission unless it falls within permitted development rights. Permitted development limits apply to size and position. Check with Coventry City Council before proceeding.

faqs.q: Do I need building regulations approval for a new kitchen?
faqs.a: The kitchen installation itself does not require building regulations approval. However, associated work including new electrical circuits (Part P) and structural changes does require notification or approval under building regulations.

faqs.q: My house is in a conservation area — does that affect my kitchen?
faqs.a: Being in a conservation area typically affects external changes rather than interior works. However, if your property is also listed, Listed Building Consent applies to interior works. Confirm with Coventry City Council if you are uncertain.

faqs.q: Can I fit a kitchen in a garage without planning permission?
faqs.a: Converting a garage to habitable use, including fitting a kitchen, typically requires planning permission and building regulations approval. This is more complex than fitting a kitchen in an existing habitable room. Take professional advice before proceeding.

title: What Qualifications Does a Kitchen Fitter Need?
metaDescription: No mandatory qualification is required to fit kitchens in the UK. NVQ Level 2 or 3 is common. Experience and portfolio of completed work are the best quality indicators.
answer: There is no mandatory qualification to fit kitchens in the UK. Experienced fitters typically hold NVQ Level 2 or 3 in Carpentry or Kitchen Fitting. Experience and a portfolio of completed kitchens are the best quality indicators. Always ask to see examples of previous work.
bodyHtml:
NVQ Qualifications Explained

There is no legal requirement for a kitchen fitter to hold a specific qualification in the UK. The most relevant qualifications are NVQ Level 2 or Level 3 in Carpentry and Joinery, or a dedicated Kitchen Installation qualification. These are competency-based qualifications assessed in the workplace. Many experienced fitters completed traditional apprenticeships rather than NVQ routes, particularly those who have been working for more than 20 years. Pindi Sahota has 30 years of practical fitting experience.

What to Look For Instead of Qualifications

Qualifications are a starting point, not a guarantee of quality. The most reliable quality indicators are a strong portfolio of recently completed kitchens, verifiable customer references, public liability insurance, a clear pre-installation survey process, and a fixed written quote. A fitter who offers these things without prompting is demonstrating professional standards that matter far more than a certificate on the wall.

Questions to Ask Any Fitter

Before engaging a kitchen fitter, ask: Can I see examples of similar kitchens you have installed? Are you insured with public liability cover? Do you carry out a pre-installation survey? Will you provide a fixed written quote? What happens if something goes wrong? A confident professional will answer all of these questions clearly and without hesitation.

faqs.q: Does a kitchen fitter need to be registered with any trade body?
faqs.a: There is no mandatory trade body registration for kitchen fitters in the UK. Some fitters are members of voluntary bodies such as TrustMark or Which? Trusted Traders, which provide additional consumer protection but are not legally required.

faqs.q: Does a kitchen fitter need public liability insurance?
faqs.a: Public liability insurance is not legally required but is essential. Any professional fitter should carry it. It protects you if the fitter causes accidental damage to your property during installation. Always ask for evidence of cover.

faqs.q: Is NVQ Level 2 or Level 3 better for kitchen fitting?
faqs.a: NVQ Level 3 represents a higher level of competency than Level 2 and is often associated with greater technical ability. However, years of practical experience on a wide variety of kitchen installations is a more useful measure of real-world quality.

faqs.q: How do I verify a fitter's experience?
faqs.a: Ask to see a portfolio of completed work, ideally kitchens similar in size and complexity to yours. Ask for references from previous customers and follow them up. A fitter with a genuine track record will welcome this scrutiny.

title: What Is a Kitchen Survey and Why Do You Need One?
metaDescription: A kitchen survey checks dimensions, services, levels and walls, then provides a fixed quote. Install My Kitchen charges £195, credited back on booking across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: A kitchen installation survey is a professional assessment of your room and kitchen plan. The surveyor checks dimensions, service positions, floor levels, wall conditions, and access routes, then provides a fixed installation quote. It prevents costly surprises on installation day.
bodyHtml:
What the Surveyor Checks

A thorough pre-installation survey covers every element that could affect your kitchen installation. This includes measuring the room and checking the plan against actual dimensions; assessing floor levels across the full run of base units; checking wall conditions for plumb, flat surfaces, and any obstacles behind plaster; confirming first-fix plumbing and electrical positions; reviewing appliance specifications against housing dimensions; and checking access routes for delivery and installation. Each of these checks prevents a problem that would otherwise only emerge on installation day.

What Problems Surveys Catch

Common issues identified at survey include: kitchen plans that do not fit the actual room dimensions; floors with significant falls that require additional levelling; walls with hidden pipes or cables in planned fixing positions; appliances that are incompatible with their housings; and access routes too narrow for tall or wide units to pass through. Identifying any of these before installation day saves significant time, cost, and frustration.

The £195 Fee and How It Is Credited Back

Install My Kitchen charges £195 for a pre-installation survey across Coventry and Warwickshire. This fee is credited back in full when you book the installation following the survey. The survey fee reflects the time, expertise, and detailed report produced. It is the most important £195 you can spend on a kitchen costing several thousand pounds. Call Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836 to book.

faqs.q: How long does a kitchen survey take?
faqs.a: A thorough pre-installation survey typically takes 1–2 hours depending on kitchen size and complexity. This allows time to check every element carefully and discuss the plan with you in detail.

faqs.q: Can a fitter quote without a survey?
faqs.a: A fitter can offer an approximate estimate without a survey, but a fixed quote requires a visit. Any fixed price provided without a survey visit transfers risk to you — the fitter will qualify it with exclusions that may emerge on installation day.

faqs.q: What do I need to have ready before the survey?
faqs.a: Have your kitchen plan and order confirmation available. If possible, have appliance specification sheets to hand. Know where your current plumbing and electrical service positions are. The more information you can provide, the more useful the survey will be.

faqs.q: What happens if the survey identifies a problem with my kitchen plan?
faqs.a: We discuss any issues with you during or immediately after the survey visit. In most cases problems have practical solutions. Where a plan needs changing, we explain the options so you can make an informed decision before any installation begins.

title: Why Use an Independent Kitchen Fitter?
metaDescription: Independent kitchen fitters work for you, not the retailer. Flexible scheduling, competitive pricing and personal accountability. Install My Kitchen is entirely independent.
answer: An independent kitchen fitter works for you, not the retailer. They offer flexible scheduling, competitive pricing, and personal accountability. They have no incentive to rush and no retailer quotas to meet. Install My Kitchen is entirely independent — our only measure of success is your finished kitchen.
bodyHtml:
What Independent Means in Practice

An independent kitchen fitter has no commercial relationship with any kitchen retailer. They do not receive referral fees, do not promote specific brands, and are not subject to retailer installation standards or timescales. This independence means their sole obligation is to you. They will raise concerns about your kitchen plan if they see potential problems, recommend modifications if something will not work, and take the time needed to do the job correctly — none of which is guaranteed with a retailer-arranged installation service.

Scheduling Flexibility

Retailer installation services operate at scale and typically assign installation dates with limited flexibility. An independent fitter can work around your household's schedule, co-ordinate directly with your plumber and electrician, and adapt the programme if circumstances change. This personal flexibility is particularly valuable in renovation projects where multiple trades are involved and timescales are tight.

Local Accountability

When you use an independent local fitter, you have a direct relationship with the person doing the work. If something needs attention after handover, you contact them directly — not a national call centre. Install My Kitchen is based at 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry, CV6 2AH. Pindi Sahota has 30 years of local experience and a reputation built entirely on the quality of completed work.

faqs.q: Is an independent fitter more expensive than using the retailer's service?
faqs.a: Not always. Independent fitters often offer more competitive pricing than retailer installation services, which include significant overhead and margin. A like-for-like comparison following a pre-installation survey typically shows independent fitting is competitive or cheaper.

faqs.q: What happens if an independent fitter makes a mistake?
faqs.a: A professional independent fitter provides a workmanship warranty and carries public liability insurance. Any installation error should be corrected by the fitter at no charge. This direct accountability is one of the key advantages of using an independent professional.

faqs.q: Can an independent fitter install any brand of kitchen?
faqs.a: Yes. Independent kitchen fitters are brand-agnostic. Install My Kitchen installs Wren, Howdens, Magnet, IKEA, and all major UK kitchen brands. The fitting process is broadly similar across brands — experience is what varies.

faqs.q: How do I know an independent fitter is trustworthy?
faqs.a: Ask for references, view a portfolio of completed work, confirm public liability insurance, and check that they carry out a pre-installation survey before quoting. These are the hallmarks of a professional independent fitter.

title: How to Find a Kitchen Fitter Near Me
metaDescription: Find a reliable kitchen fitter near you by checking for a pre-installation survey, fixed quote, and completed examples. Install My Kitchen covers all Coventry postcodes and Warwickshire.
answer: To find a reliable local kitchen fitter: confirm they offer a pre-installation survey, ask for a fixed written quote, and ask to see completed examples. Avoid anyone who quotes without visiting your home. Install My Kitchen covers all Coventry postcodes and Warwickshire.
bodyHtml:
The Survey Test for Quality

The single most reliable test of a kitchen fitter's professionalism is whether they carry out a pre-installation survey before providing a fixed quote. Any fitter who quotes over the phone or by email without visiting your home is either unable to spot problems or unwilling to spend the time. Either way, this is a significant warning sign. A professional fitter insists on a survey because they know the risks of installing a kitchen without seeing the room first.

What a Proper Quote Looks Like

A professional kitchen fitting quote is a written document that specifies exactly what is included: cabinetry installation, worktop fitting, appliance installation, strip-out if included, and any exclusions. It should state a fixed total price — not just a day rate — and a programme of work. Any quote that is vague about scope or excludes common elements without explanation should prompt further questions before you sign.

Our Coverage in Coventry and Warwickshire

Install My Kitchen covers all Coventry postcodes — CV1 through CV6 and beyond — as well as surrounding Warwickshire towns including Kenilworth, Leamington Spa, Nuneaton, Rugby, Bedworth, and Meriden. Pindi Sahota has worked in homes across this area for 30 years. Call 07399 651836 or visit 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry, CV6 2AH to start your project.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book a kitchen fitter?
faqs.a: Popular fitters book 4–10 weeks in advance, particularly in spring and autumn. It is sensible to book your survey as soon as your kitchen order is confirmed so installation can be timed around your delivery date.

faqs.q: Should I get more than one fitting quote?
faqs.a: Getting two or three quotes is sensible, but only from fitters who carry out a proper pre-installation survey. Comparing quotes that have not been based on a site visit is not a meaningful comparison.

faqs.q: Are online kitchen fitter directories reliable?
faqs.a: Directories such as Checkatrade and Rated People provide a starting point. However, always apply your own verification — check reviews carefully, ask for references, and confirm insurance and survey process before engaging anyone.

faqs.q: Does Install My Kitchen cover areas outside Coventry?
faqs.a: Yes. Install My Kitchen covers all Coventry postcodes and surrounding Warwickshire, including Kenilworth, Leamington Spa, Nuneaton, Rugby, and Bedworth. Call 07399 651836 to confirm coverage for your specific location.

title: What Happens After Your Kitchen Is Delivered?
metaDescription: After delivery: check all items, report damage immediately, confirm first-fix services are complete, and contact your fitter to confirm the start date.
answer: After delivery: check every item against your order and report damage to the retailer before signing. Store units in a dry protected space. Ensure first-fix plumbing and electrical is complete before installation day. Confirm the start date with your fitter.
bodyHtml:
Checking Your Delivery Thoroughly

When your kitchen is delivered, take the time to check every item against your order before the delivery team leaves. Open every box and check for damage, missing items, and incorrect components. Check door colours and sizes match the order. Check worktop lengths and profiles. Check appliance models and specifications. This check takes time but is essential — it is far easier to resolve problems on delivery day than after the delivery team has left and items have been accepted.

Reporting Damage to the Retailer

If any damage is found, note it clearly on the delivery documentation before signing. Photograph every damaged item clearly. Contact the retailer's customer service team on delivery day, not the following day. Most major retailers including Wren, Howdens, and Magnet have clear processes for replacing damaged components and will act quickly when damage is reported promptly. Do not allow installation to begin on any damaged units.

Confirming Installation Readiness

After accepting delivery, confirm with your plumber that first-fix plumbing is complete and confirm with your electrician that first-fix electrical work is done. Store your kitchen units in a dry, protected space — damp storage can damage flat-pack components. Then contact your fitter to confirm the installation start date and advise them that delivery is complete and undamaged. Install My Kitchen's contact number is 07399 651836.

faqs.q: What if I find damage after the delivery team has left?
faqs.a: Contact the retailer immediately — the same day if at all possible. Most retailers accept damage claims on delivery day. Claims made days later are harder to resolve. Always photograph damage before moving anything.

faqs.q: Where should I store my kitchen units before installation?
faqs.a: Store units indoors in a dry space — a garage, spare room, or living room. Do not store in damp outbuildings or directly on concrete floors. Flat-pack units are particularly vulnerable to moisture damage.

faqs.q: Should I be present for delivery?
faqs.a: Yes. You or a trusted representative should be present for the full delivery to check items against the order. A delivery accepted in your absence without checking is very difficult to dispute if problems emerge later.

faqs.q: How long will my kitchen be in storage before installation?
faqs.a: Ideally, delivery should be timed as close to your installation start date as possible. If storage is needed, ensure it is in dry, protected conditions. Discuss delivery timing with your retailer and fitter at the planning stage.

title: What to Do If Kitchen Units Are Damaged on Delivery
metaDescription: Report damage to the retailer immediately before signing the delivery note. Photograph all damage. Wren, Howdens and Magnet replace damaged units promptly. Do not start installation on damaged units.
answer: Report damage to the retailer immediately on delivery day, before signing any delivery note. Photograph all damage clearly. Most major retailers including Wren and Howdens replace damaged components promptly. Do not allow installation to begin on any damaged units.
bodyHtml:
Document Damage Before Signing

The most important action if kitchen units are damaged on delivery is to document everything before signing the delivery note. Note each damaged item clearly on the paperwork — write specific descriptions rather than a general note. Photograph each damaged component from multiple angles, including close-up shots of the damage and wider shots showing the item in context. If the delivery team is present, ask them to acknowledge the damage on the paperwork. Do not sign to accept delivery as complete if damaged items are present.

How Major Retailers Handle Replacements

Wren, Howdens, Magnet, and IKEA all have customer service processes for handling damaged delivery components. When damage is reported promptly and with clear photographic evidence, most retailers replace damaged components without significant dispute. Replacement lead times vary — typically 3–10 working days depending on the brand and component. This is why damage must be reported on delivery day, not days later when your installation start date is approaching.

If Damage Is Found After Delivery

If damage is discovered after the delivery team has left, contact the retailer immediately — the same day is essential. Claims made several days after delivery are harder to resolve, particularly for damage that could have occurred during your own storage. Always keep all original packaging until the kitchen is fully installed and snagged. Install My Kitchen recommends that no installation begins on any unit that is visibly damaged.

faqs.q: Can installation start if only one or two units are damaged?
faqs.a: Installation can begin on undamaged sections of the kitchen, but damaged units should not be installed in their damaged state. Discuss with your fitter which sections can proceed while you await replacement components.

faqs.q: What if the retailer refuses to replace damaged units?
faqs.a: If you have photographic evidence and noted the damage on the delivery documentation, the retailer is legally obliged to replace or refund damaged goods under the Consumer Rights Act 2015. Escalate through the retailer's complaints process if necessary.

faqs.q: Who is responsible for damage during delivery — the retailer or the delivery company?
faqs.a: Your contract is with the retailer, not the delivery company. The retailer is responsible for ensuring goods arrive undamaged. Report to the retailer — not the delivery company — and they will handle any claim against their logistics provider.

faqs.q: Should I check worktops as well as units on delivery?
faqs.a: Yes. Worktops are particularly vulnerable to damage in transit. Check for chips, splits, and incorrect lengths or profiles. Report any worktop damage immediately — these can be the most expensive items to replace and have the longest lead times.

title: Do Kitchen Fitters Supply Worktops?
metaDescription: Install My Kitchen supplies and fits laminate and solid wood worktops. For stone worktops we co-ordinate with a specialist fabricator for templating and fitting across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: Some fitters supply worktops; others install only. Install My Kitchen supplies and fits laminate and solid wood worktops directly. For stone worktops, we co-ordinate with a specialist stone fabricator for templating and cutting.
bodyHtml:
Laminate and Solid Wood — Supplied and Fitted

Install My Kitchen supplies and fits laminate and solid wood worktops as part of our installation service. We work with a range of laminate finishes including post-formed edges and square edges, and solid oak and hardwood options. Supplying the worktop directly means we can cut and fit on the day without waiting for a separate delivery. Laminate and solid wood worktops are measured, cut, joined, and finished to a professional standard as part of the installation programme.

Stone Worktops and the Templating Process

Quartz, granite, and other stone worktops cannot be supplied directly by a kitchen fitter — they require specialist fabrication. The process involves a templating visit after the base units are installed, during which the stone fabricator creates an exact template of your worktop area. The worktop is then cut to the template in the fabricator's workshop and returned for fitting, typically 5–10 working days later. Install My Kitchen co-ordinates this process with a trusted local stone fabricator.

Worktop Cost and What Is Included

Laminate worktops supplied and fitted by Install My Kitchen are priced per linear metre including cutting, jointing, and upstand fitting. Solid wood worktops are priced similarly. Stone worktop costs depend on the material chosen and are quoted by the fabricator. All worktop costs are itemised clearly in your installation quote following the pre-installation survey. Call 07399 651836 for details.

faqs.q: Can I supply my own worktop and have Install My Kitchen fit it?
faqs.a: Yes. If you have purchased a worktop separately, we can fit it as part of the installation. Confirm the material and any special requirements at the survey stage so we have the correct tools available.

faqs.q: How long does stone worktop templating take?
faqs.a: The templating visit takes approximately 1–2 hours. The fabrication and return visit typically takes 5–10 working days. Installation must pause during this period — the programme is planned to account for this at the survey stage.

faqs.q: What is the most popular worktop choice in Coventry homes?
faqs.a: Laminate worktops remain the most popular choice due to cost-effectiveness and the wide range of finishes available. Quartz is increasingly popular in mid-range and premium kitchens. Solid wood is popular in traditional and shaker-style kitchens.

faqs.q: Can worktop joints be made invisible?
faqs.a: A well-made worktop joint on laminate or solid wood is very tight and minimally visible. Colour-matched edging strips and careful cutting technique minimise the appearance of joints. Stone worktops can be templated to minimise joints where possible.

title: Is It Worth Getting a Kitchen Survey Before Installation?
metaDescription: Yes — a kitchen survey is the most important step before installation. Costs £195, credited back on booking. Prevents expensive surprises. Book with Install My Kitchen across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: Yes — always. A pre-installation survey is the most important step before any kitchen installation. It identifies problems before they become expensive, provides a fixed quote, and ensures your plan works in your actual room. Our survey costs £195, credited back on booking.
bodyHtml:
What Surveys Typically Find

A pre-installation kitchen survey regularly identifies issues that would cause significant problems on installation day. The most common findings include kitchen plans that do not fit the actual room dimensions due to measurement errors; floors with falls that require additional levelling work; walls that are out of plumb and require compensating when fitting tall units; service positions that do not align with planned appliance locations; and appliance specifications that are incompatible with their housings in the kitchen plan. Finding these issues before installation day is the difference between a smooth project and an expensive delay.

The Cost vs the Risk of Skipping It

A pre-installation survey costs £195 with Install My Kitchen. Compare this with the cost of an installation day lost to a problem that a survey would have identified — typically £300–£450 in fitter time alone, plus any remedial materials. Skipping a survey to save £195 on a kitchen costing £5,000–£20,000 is a poor risk calculation. The survey pays for itself the first time it prevents a problem, and it is credited back in full on booking.

How the £195 Credit Works

Install My Kitchen charges £195 for the pre-installation survey. When you book your installation following the survey, this £195 is credited in full against your installation price. The £195 survey fee is effectively offset against your installation — you only pay it if you choose not to proceed after the survey. Call Pindi Sahota on 07399 651836 to arrange your survey.

faqs.q: Can I book installation without a survey?
faqs.a: Install My Kitchen requires a pre-installation survey before booking any kitchen installation. This protects you and us. We are not able to provide a fixed installation price without visiting your home.

faqs.q: What if the survey finds a problem I did not know about?
faqs.a: We discuss any findings with you immediately and explain the options. In the vast majority of cases, problems have practical solutions that can be planned before installation day. The survey report is your planning tool.

faqs.q: How soon before installation should I book the survey?
faqs.a: Book the survey as soon as your kitchen order is confirmed and you have a delivery date. This gives maximum time to resolve any issues identified and to confirm an installation start date that works for all trades.

faqs.q: Is the survey report written or verbal?
faqs.a: The survey produces a written fixed quote and installation programme. Key findings are discussed with you in person during the visit. You receive a clear written document to work from and share with your other trades.

title: How Do Kitchen Fitters Level Kitchen Units?
metaDescription: Kitchen fitters level units using adjustable feet, spirit levels and laser levels. The first unit sets the reference for the whole kitchen. From 30 years fitting in Coventry homes.
answer: Kitchen fitters level units using adjustable feet, spirit levels, and laser levels. Packing pieces compensate for unlevel floors. Getting the first base unit perfectly level sets the reference for the entire kitchen — every subsequent unit is levelled from this point.
bodyHtml:
Adjustable Feet and Laser Levels

Modern kitchen base units come fitted with adjustable plastic feet that can be raised or lowered to compensate for uneven floors. A professional fitter uses a combination of a spirit level and a laser level to establish a consistent working height across the full run of base units. The laser level projects a horizontal reference line across the entire room, making it possible to level units at both ends of a long run precisely. This technique is far more accurate than a spirit level alone and is standard practice for experienced fitters.

Why the First Unit Matters Most

The first base unit installed sets the reference height for the entire kitchen. Every subsequent unit is levelled from this point — so any error in the first unit propagates through the whole run. With 30 years of fitting experience, Pindi Sahota takes particular care with the first unit, confirming it is perfectly level in all directions before proceeding. This attention at the start of the day saves significant time in adjustment later.

Uneven Floors in Coventry Homes

Many homes in Coventry — particularly Victorian and inter-war terraced and semi-detached properties — have floors with significant falls or irregularities. These require more careful levelling work and sometimes additional packing beneath adjustable feet. A pre-installation survey identifies floor levels before installation day so the programme accounts for any extra time needed. This is one of the most valuable elements of the survey for older properties.

faqs.q: What happens if my floor falls more than the adjustable feet can compensate for?
faqs.a: In cases of extreme floor fall, additional packing blocks are used beneath the adjustable feet to raise the cabinet to the correct height. In severe cases, a plinth can be built up to conceal the gap. This is identified at the survey stage.

faqs.q: Do wall units also need to be levelled?
faqs.a: Yes. Wall units are hung to a consistent height using a laser level reference line. Getting wall units level and at the correct height relative to base units is essential for a consistent finished appearance and correct appliance fit.

faqs.q: Does an uneven floor affect the worktop?
faqs.a: Yes. If base units are not levelled correctly, the worktop will not sit flat. An uneven worktop is both visually obvious and practically problematic. This is why accurate levelling of base units is the most critical part of the installation.

faqs.q: Do adjustable feet get hidden after installation?
faqs.a: Yes. Plinths are fitted around the base of the kitchen after all units are levelled and worktops are fitted. The plinths conceal the adjustable feet completely, giving the kitchen a built-in appearance.

title: What Is Kitchen Snagging and What Should Be Checked?
metaDescription: Snagging is the final inspection stage — checking door alignment, drawer operation, handles, plinths and worktop joints before handover. Install My Kitchen includes full snagging on every installation.
answer: Kitchen snagging is the final inspection stage where every element is checked and adjusted before handover. This includes door alignment, drawer operation, handle positioning, plinth fit, worktop joints, and appliance function. Thorough snagging separates professional installation from a rushed job.
bodyHtml:
What Snagging Covers

Kitchen snagging is a systematic final inspection carried out after all installation work is complete. It covers every element of the finished kitchen: door alignment in all planes (top, bottom, side, gap consistency); drawer operation and soft-close function; handle positioning and consistency; plinth fit and scribing to the floor; cornice and pelmet alignment; worktop joints and upstand silicone; sink and appliance fit and function; and any areas of visible damage or marking caused during installation. Nothing should be left for the homeowner to discover after the fitter has left.

The Snagging Checklist

A professional snagging checklist typically covers 20–40 individual checks depending on kitchen size. Doors are opened and closed repeatedly to check hinge alignment. Drawers are fully extended and operated. Every handle is checked for consistent height and alignment. The worktop is inspected for level and joint quality. Upstands are checked for consistent silicone bead and full adhesion. Appliances are tested for basic function. The floor is checked for plinth gaps. Any fault found is corrected before the homeowner is called for handover.

Our Sign-Off Process

Install My Kitchen carries out a full snagging check on every installation before calling the homeowner for final inspection. We walk through the kitchen together, and any remaining adjustments are made on the spot. Installation is not complete until you are satisfied. Call 07399 651836 to discuss your installation with Pindi Sahota at 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry, CV6 2AH.

faqs.q: What is the most common snagging issue in a new kitchen?
faqs.a: Door alignment is the most frequently adjusted item at snagging. Modern concealed hinges have three-axis adjustment and allow for precise alignment — a skilled fitter can correct most alignment issues in minutes.

faqs.q: How long does snagging take?
faqs.a: A thorough snagging check on a medium kitchen takes 1–3 hours. Larger kitchens or those with many appliances take longer. Snagging time is built into the installation programme — it is not an afterthought.

faqs.q: Can I snag my kitchen myself after the fitter leaves?
faqs.a: You can and should inspect your kitchen carefully after installation. However, snagging should primarily be carried out by the fitter before handover. Issues are far easier to address while the fitter is still on site and has all tools available.

faqs.q: What if I find a snagging issue after the fitter has left?
faqs.a: Contact Install My Kitchen and we will arrange a return visit to correct any genuine installation issues. Our workmanship warranty covers the installation. Most minor adjustments such as door alignment can be corrected at a short return visit.

title: What Is a Kitchen Plinth?
metaDescription: A kitchen plinth (kickboard) covers the gap between cabinet bases and the floor, hiding adjustable feet. Plinths are included in Install My Kitchen's standard installation service.
answer: A kitchen plinth, also called a kickboard, is the decorative panel covering the gap between the base of your kitchen units and the floor. It hides adjustable feet, protects the base of units, and gives the kitchen a built-in appearance. Plinths are included in our standard installation.
bodyHtml:
What a Plinth Does

A kitchen plinth — also known as a kickboard — serves both a functional and aesthetic purpose. Functionally, it conceals the adjustable feet used to level the base units and prevents debris from accumulating under the cabinets. Aesthetically, it creates a continuous visual base to the kitchen, giving it a built-in, furniture-like appearance. Without plinths, the exposed feet and underside of the cabinets give the kitchen an unfinished look. Plinths are one of the last elements fitted during installation and make a significant difference to the finished appearance.

Materials and Fitting

Plinths are typically supplied by the kitchen manufacturer in the same finish as the cabinet doors and supplied in standard lengths. They are cut to length and clipped into place using plinth clips fixed to the adjustable feet. On straight runs, plinth fitting is straightforward. External corners require a mitre cut. The plinth height is matched to the gap between the floor and the underside of the unit, which is set during the levelling process.

Scribing Plinths to Uneven Floors

Where a floor is significantly uneven, the plinth must be scribed — cut to follow the exact profile of the floor — to eliminate visible gaps. Scribing requires a profile gauge and careful cutting with a jigsaw. It is a skilled task that is particularly common in older Coventry properties with stone or uneven solid floors. Install My Kitchen includes all plinth fitting and scribing in our standard installation service.

faqs.q: Are plinths included with my kitchen when I buy it?
faqs.a: Most kitchen retailers include plinth panels with the kitchen order, but check your order confirmation. Plinths are ordered to match your door colour and finish. Install My Kitchen fits all plinths supplied with your kitchen as part of the standard installation.

faqs.q: Can I fit my own plinths after the kitchen is installed?
faqs.a: Technically yes, but plinths are best fitted by the kitchen fitter who has set the unit heights and knows the exact gap to the floor. Scribing to an uneven floor in particular requires experience and the correct tools.

faqs.q: What is the standard plinth height for a kitchen?
faqs.a: Standard plinth height is typically 150mm, corresponding to the standard adjustable foot height range. The actual installed height may vary slightly depending on floor levels and the height at which units have been set.

faqs.q: Can I get a different colour plinth to my doors?
faqs.a: This depends on your kitchen supplier. Some brands offer plinths in contrasting colours or materials. Discuss this with your retailer at the design stage — plinth colour choice is often overlooked until it is too late to change.

title: What Is a Kitchen Carcass?
metaDescription: A kitchen carcass is the structural box forming each cabinet. Available in flat-pack (IKEA) or rigid pre-assembled (Wren, Howdens, Magnet). Carcass quality determines long-term structural integrity.
answer: A kitchen carcass is the structural box forming each cabinet — the sides, back, base, and top. Doors, drawers, and shelves attach to the carcass. Available in flat-pack (IKEA) or rigid pre-assembled (Wren, Howdens, Magnet). Carcass quality determines long-term structural integrity.
bodyHtml:
What a Carcass Is Made Of

A kitchen carcass is the structural box that forms the body of each kitchen cabinet. It consists of two side panels, a back panel, a base, and — on base units — a top rail. In most modern kitchens, carcasses are made from moisture-resistant (MR) chipboard or MDF with a melamine-faced interior. The thickness of the carcass panels varies between brands and price points — thicker panels (18mm or more) produce a more rigid, durable carcass than thinner economy alternatives. Doors, drawer fronts, shelves, and hinges all attach to the carcass.

Flat-Pack vs Rigid Pre-Assembled

Kitchen carcasses are supplied in two main forms. Flat-pack carcasses — most commonly associated with IKEA — are supplied as panels for assembly on site. Rigid pre-assembled carcasses — used by Howdens, Magnet, and Wren's higher ranges — arrive fully assembled and ready to install. Rigid carcasses are quicker to install and generally more structurally consistent. Flat-pack carcasses require careful assembly but can be carried into the home more easily through narrow access routes.

How Carcass Quality Affects Installation

The quality of a kitchen carcass directly affects how it behaves during and after installation. Good-quality carcasses hold fixings securely, resist moisture from cooking and cleaning, and maintain their structural integrity for decades. Lower-quality carcasses can swell, distort, or fail to hold hinges and drawer runners over time. Pindi Sahota has 30 years of fitting experience across all carcass types and can advise on quality differences during the pre-installation survey.

faqs.q: Does a more expensive carcass mean a better kitchen?
faqs.a: Generally yes, up to a point. Thicker panels, better edge banding, and higher-quality internal fittings all contribute to longevity. However, the most expensive carcass from a budget brand may be inferior to a mid-range carcass from a quality manufacturer.

faqs.q: Can flat-pack carcasses be as strong as rigid ones?
faqs.a: A well-assembled flat-pack carcass using cam locks and good-quality connectors can be very strong. IKEA METOD, for example, has an excellent structural record. The key is careful assembly — a poorly assembled flat-pack carcass is structurally weak.

faqs.q: What thickness should a kitchen carcass panel be?
faqs.a: 18mm is considered a solid standard thickness for kitchen carcass panels. Some premium brands use 19mm or thicker. Panels below 15mm should be viewed with caution for base units carrying worktop loads.

faqs.q: How long should a kitchen carcass last?
faqs.a: A good quality kitchen carcass installed correctly should last 20–30 years or more. The most common failure points are hinges, drawer runners, and internal fittings rather than the carcass itself. Moisture damage from leaks is the most common cause of early carcass failure.
